title: Render hook decisions for supported protocols
description:
## Scope
Render hook decisions for each supported protocol and produce human-readable denial guidance on stderr.

## Deliverables
- protocol-specific stdout rendering for Claude Code, Gemini CLI, and GitHub Copilot
- silent allow output on stderr
- denied output with reason, severity, confidence, and remediation text

## Out of Scope
- policy evaluation
- audit log persistence
- CLI subcommands outside hook mode

## Acceptance Criteria
- unit tests cover allow and deny output for each supported protocol
- denied output includes a spine-tool style remediation hint when available
- allow output does not emit noisy stderr text

title: Recognize authorized spine tool invocations
description:
## Scope
Recognize authorized spine tool invocations inside Bash tool input so the evaluator can allow approved subprocess processing.

## Deliverables
- spine tool detection for configured tool names by basename or full path
- target-path extraction for the sensitive file the spine tool is acting on
- handling for piped commands where the first stage is the spine tool invocation

## Out of Scope
- final allow or deny policy decisions
- generic Bash parsing beyond what is needed for spine matching
- hook protocol parsing

## Acceptance Criteria
- unit tests cover a basic match, a full-path match, a piped match, and a non-spine command
- the matcher returns the detected tool name and target path
- detection does not mark unrelated commands as spine tools

comments.author: WhiteHeron
comments.text: Implemented src/spine.rs with authorized spine subprocess detection by basename or full command path, left-side pipeline handling, and first-target-path extraction. Added unit coverage for basic, full-path, piped, and non-spine cases. Verified with cargo fmt --check, cargo test, and cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings.

title: Implement core path-based packs: filesystem and database
description:
## Scope
Implement the always-sensitive built-in packs whose classification is based on path and file type rather than project-protected directories.

## Deliverables
- `core.filesystem` matching common sensitive paths such as `.env`, `.ssh/`, `*.pem`, and `*.key`
- `data.database` matching SQLite, DuckDB, and dump-like database artifacts
- deterministic pack metadata with pack name, severity, and confidence

## Out of Scope
- registry and dispatch infrastructure
- protected-directory packs such as CSV and XML
- heuristic credentials or compliance packs

## Acceptance Criteria
- unit tests cover representative matches and representative non-matches for both packs
- database artifacts are treated as globally sensitive, not protected-directory-only
- the pack outputs conform to the shared `SensitivityResult` contract from the registry bead

title: CLI subcommands: scan and packs
description:
## Scope
Add the discovery-oriented commands for classifying directories and listing the built-in pack inventory.

## Deliverables
- `veil scan <dir>` to classify files under a directory
- `veil packs` to list built-in pack names and short descriptions
- human-readable and `--json` output modes

## Out of Scope
- single-path explain output
- config and audit introspection
- hook installation commands

## Acceptance Criteria
- `veil scan` can walk a small fixture directory and report at least one sensitive and one non-sensitive example
- `veil packs` lists the built-in packs present in the registry
- tests cover both text output and `--json` output

title: Parse hook stdin into a protocol-neutral HookInput
description:
## Scope
Parse hook stdin payloads from supported clients into a protocol-neutral `HookInput` that downstream code can consume.

## Deliverables
- `src/hook.rs`
- protocol detection for Claude Code, Gemini CLI, and GitHub Copilot payloads
- normalized tool kind plus raw tool input capture
- clear error handling for unsupported payloads

## Out of Scope
- path extraction
- allow or deny evaluation
- stdout or stderr rendering

## Acceptance Criteria
- unit tests cover one valid payload from each supported protocol
- unsupported or malformed payloads return a graceful error without panic
- the normalized value preserves the raw tool input needed by downstream extractors

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Starting work on bd-1cp. Active file reservations: src/lib.rs, src/types.rs, and planned new file src/hook.rs only. Agent mail server binary exists locally, but MCP mail tools are not exposed in this session, so coordination is via Beads comments.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Starting work. Reserving only active files: Cargo.toml, src/lib.rs, src/types.rs, and new src/hook.rs for protocol-neutral hook parsing and tests.

comments.author: Zac
comments.text: DarkForest started work on hook stdin parsing. Investigating protocol payloads and will reserve only active files.

title: Implement path-based heuristic packs: credentials and compliance
description:
## Scope
Implement the built-in heuristic packs using path and filename conventions only, without opening file contents.

## Deliverables
- `core.credentials` for credentials-bearing config and token-like targets
- compliance-oriented packs for financial, PII, and HIPAA-style artifacts using path, filename, and extension heuristics
- clear pack metadata so `explain` style commands can report why a match occurred

## Out of Scope
- reading file contents
- registry and dispatch infrastructure
- path extraction
- evaluator policy resolution

## Acceptance Criteria
- unit tests cover representative positive and negative cases for each heuristic pack
- no pack requires opening or sampling file contents to classify a path
- false-positive guard cases are included for common safe files such as docs and source code
- pack ordering does not create unstable or conflicting results for the same path

title: Utility subcommands: test, explain, scan, packs, config, doctor (src/cli.rs)
description: Add CLI subcommands to existing clap structure: 'veil test <path>' checks decision, 'veil explain <path>' shows matching pack/reason, 'veil scan <dir>' classifies directory, 'veil packs' lists packs, 'veil config' shows resolved config, 'veil audit' shows recent log entries, 'veil doctor' validates installation. File: extends src/cli.rs. Acceptance: each subcommand produces output, --json flag works.
close_reason: Split into smaller CLI subcommand beads

title: Publish Homebrew formula for veil in cmdrvl/tap
description:
## Scope
Publish the `veil` Homebrew formula in `cmdrvl/tap` once release artifacts are stable.

## Deliverables
- formula definition pointing at the release tarballs for each supported platform
- `bin.install` for `veil`
- a Homebrew test block that verifies `veil --version`

## Out of Scope
- release workflow generation
- shell installer logic
- repo-local Cargo build behavior

## Acceptance Criteria
- the formula resolves the correct artifact URL for each supported platform
- Homebrew can install the binary and run the test block successfully
- formula metadata matches the release artifact naming contract produced by the release workflow

title: Extract candidate paths from simple Bash read commands
description:
## Scope
Extract candidate file paths from simple Bash read commands that name files directly.

## Deliverables
- support for direct file-reading commands such as `cat`, `head`, `tail`, `less`, `more`, and `bat`
- quoted path handling
- multiple file argument handling
- simple pipe handling where the left side reads a file and later stages post-process output

## Out of Scope
- heredocs
- inline interpreter snippets such as `python -c` and `node -e`
- path hardening after extraction

## Acceptance Criteria
- unit tests cover direct commands, quoted paths, multiple file arguments, and simple piped forms
- non-read commands such as `ls`, `git`, and `cd` return zero candidates
- false positives are avoided for commands that inspect metadata without reading file contents

comments.author: WhiteHeron
comments.text: Implemented simple Bash read-command extraction in `src/extract.rs` using conservative shell-token parsing for direct readers (`cat`, `head`, `tail`, `less`, `more`, `bat`). Added support for quoted paths, multiple file operands, left-side pipeline reads, and defensive non-reader rejection. Verified with `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo test`, and `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`.,workdir:/Users/zac/Source/cmdrvl/veil,max_output_tokens:4000,yield_time_ms:1000}},{

title: Scaffold Cargo crate, toolchain, and shared types
description:
## Scope
Create the initial Rust crate for `veil` and only the minimal project skeleton needed to unblock feature work.

## Deliverables
- `Cargo.toml` with package metadata, pinned dependencies, and the release profile from AGENTS
- `rust-toolchain.toml`
- `src/main.rs`, `src/lib.rs`, `src/types.rs`, and `LICENSE`
- a minimal binary entrypoint that compiles cleanly and gives downstream beads a stable crate layout

## Out of Scope
- CI workflow
- protocol parsing
- CLI command surface
- real evaluation logic
- installer and release automation

## Acceptance Criteria
- `cargo build` succeeds
- `cargo fmt --check` succeeds
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` succeeds
- `cargo test` succeeds
- the scaffold defines the shared types other beads will build on without placeholder logic from later beads
- the scaffold does not include CI workflow files or partial implementations owned by other beads
design:
## Implementation Notes
- Keep shared domain structs in `src/types.rs` as data-only types with no policy logic
- `main.rs` should stay thin and delegate to library code
- Include `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]` and keep dependencies pinned and minimal
acceptance_criteria:
## Acceptance Criteria
- `cargo build` succeeds
- `cargo fmt --check` succeeds
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` succeeds
- `cargo test` succeeds
- Running the binary with valid JSON on stdin exits 0 and emits a stub allow response
- The scaffold does not include CI workflow files or partial implementations owned by other beads
notes:
## Definition of Done
The repo has a clean Rust scaffold that downstream beads can build on without renaming or reworking core crate structure.

title: Detect inline-script and heredoc file reads in Bash input
description:
## Scope
Detect file reads hidden inside heredocs and inline interpreter snippets in Bash tool input.

## Deliverables
- detection for heredoc-fed file reads and redirects
- detection for inline interpreters such as `python -c`, `node -e`, `perl -e`, and `ruby -e`
- extraction of candidate file paths from these forms when they read files

## Out of Scope
- simple direct read commands already covered by the basic Bash extractor
- evaluator policy decisions
- path hardening after extraction

## Acceptance Criteria
- golden-style tests cover heredoc, `python -c`, `node -e`, `perl -e`, and `ruby -e` examples
- safe commands such as `echo`, `mkdir`, and `git status` do not produce false positives
- nested quoting edge cases are handled without panic

comments.author: WhiteHeron
comments.text: Starting bd-2aj. Reserving only src/extract.rs to extend the Bash extractor for heredoc-fed reads, inline interpreter snippets, and redirected input reads without changing downstream policy code.

comments.author: WhiteHeron
comments.text: Implemented advanced Bash read extraction in src/extract.rs for input redirects, inline interpreter snippets (, , , ), and heredoc-fed shell/interpreter bodies. Added focused tests covering heredoc reads, redirect reads, inline interpreter reads, safe-command false positives, and nested quoting. Verified with cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test, and ubs --diff .

title: Create CLI shell and command dispatch
description:
## Scope
Create the shared CLI surface and command dispatch layer that both hook mode and operator commands build on.

## Deliverables
- clap parser and command enum in `src/cli.rs`
- `--help` and `--version` behavior
- dispatch that selects hook mode when no subcommand is provided and routes explicit subcommands to their handlers
- tests for argument parsing and command selection

## Out of Scope
- hook-mode policy evaluation and IO pipeline
- implementation of operator subcommands beyond dispatch hooks
- release and installer automation

## Acceptance Criteria
- `veil --help` and `veil --version` work from the compiled binary
- parsing with no subcommand selects hook mode
- parsing each defined subcommand selects the expected command variant
- downstream command beads can add behavior without reworking top-level CLI structure

comments.author: Zac
comments.text: AmberCompass starting bd-2f1. Reserving only Cargo.toml, src/main.rs, and new src/cli.rs for the clap-based CLI shell and dispatch layer.

comments.author: Zac
comments.text: AmberCompass completed bd-2f1. Added clap-based parsing in `src/cli.rs`, wired `src/main.rs` to select hook mode with no subcommand and dispatch operator commands by name, and added parser tests covering hook-mode selection plus every defined subcommand. Verified with `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo test`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo run -- --help`, and `cargo run -- --version`.

title: Write JSONL audit records outside the repo
description:
## Scope
Append structured audit records for access attempts to a JSONL log outside the repo.

## Deliverables
- `src/audit.rs`
- default audit path resolution to user state outside the working tree
- append-only JSONL writer with directory auto-creation
- stable record shape for timestamp, tool, path, decision, reason, sensitivity, confidence, and session metadata

## Out of Scope
- deciding whether an access should be allowed
- CLI rendering of audit entries
- log rotation or retention policy

## Acceptance Criteria
- unit tests write and read back a record successfully
- the default path resolves outside the repo
- missing parent directories are created automatically
- multiple writes append valid JSONL records rather than overwriting prior entries

comments.author: Zac
comments.text: AmberCompass starting bd-2f9. Reserving only src/audit.rs and src/lib.rs for the JSONL audit writer and module export.

comments.author: AmberCompass
comments.text: AmberCompass starting bd-2f9. Reserving only src/audit.rs and src/lib.rs to add the JSONL audit writer, default outside-repo path resolution, and unit coverage.

comments.author: AmberCompass
comments.text: Validated bd-2f9 on the current tree with cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test, and ubs --staged .. The audit writer commit is now the current HEAD and is being pushed before bead closure.

title: Sensitivity pack registry and classifier dispatch
description:
## Scope
Create the shared sensitivity-pack registry and dispatch path that runs registered packs in deterministic order.

## Deliverables
- `src/packs/` registry module
- a pack interface or equivalent data contract for built-in packs
- deterministic dispatch and merge semantics for pack results
- a classifier entry point that returns `SensitivityResult` values with pack metadata

## Out of Scope
- individual path and heuristic rules for specific packs
- policy resolution after a match
- hook output formatting

## Acceptance Criteria
- tests with fake packs prove deterministic ordering and stable result selection
- the registry can register the built-in pack set and dispatch over it
- classifier output includes pack name, severity, confidence, and the `directory_sensitive` flag

title: Load layered configuration and merge overrides
description:
## Scope
Load the resolved `veil` configuration from defaults, config files, and environment overrides.

## Deliverables
- `src/config.rs`
- support for precedence `env > project > user > system > defaults`
- partial merge behavior for nested sections such as `sensitivity`, `allowlist`, `policy`, and `spine`
- clear surfaced errors for invalid config input

## Out of Scope
- evaluator decision logic
- CLI formatting of resolved config output
- path classification itself

## Acceptance Criteria
- unit tests cover each layer individually and the full precedence order
- missing config files are ignored cleanly
- invalid TOML produces a clear error
- environment overrides work for at least protected paths and policy settings

comments.author: AmberCompass
comments.text: AmberCompass starting bd-2ph. Reserving only Cargo.toml, src/config.rs, and src/lib.rs to implement layered config loading and merge behavior without touching the active src/extract.rs bead.

comments.author: AmberCompass
comments.text: Implemented layered config loading in src/config.rs with precedence env > project > user > system > defaults, partial nested-section merges, clean missing-file handling, clear invalid-TOML errors, and coverage for each layer plus full precedence. Validation passed with cargo fmt, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test, and ubs --staged . on the combined shared snapshot.

title: Implement protected-directory data packs: tabular and XML
description:
## Scope
Implement the built-in packs that only become sensitive when the path is inside a configured protected directory.

## Deliverables
- `data.tabular` matching CSV, TSV, and parquet-like tabular files
- `data.xml` matching XML filings such as NPORT and EDGAR artifacts
- `directory_sensitive=true` semantics on returned matches

## Out of Scope
- registry and dispatch infrastructure
- global filesystem or database packs
- heuristic credentials or compliance packs

## Acceptance Criteria
- unit tests cover representative tabular and XML paths
- matching results from both packs set `directory_sensitive=true`
- representative files outside protected directories can be allowed once evaluated with directory context

title: CLI subcommands: test, explain, scan, packs, doctor, install
description: Implement CLI with clap: 'veil test <path>' checks if file would be blocked, 'veil explain <path>' shows why sensitive, 'veil scan <dir>' classifies directory contents, 'veil packs' lists sensitivity packs, 'veil config' shows resolved config, 'veil audit' shows recent log, 'veil doctor' self-tests, 'veil install/uninstall' manages settings.json hooks. Model after dcg's cli.rs.
close_reason: Split into core CLI and utility subcommands

title: Canonicalize and harden extracted paths before matching
description:
## Scope
Canonicalize and harden extracted paths before allowlist and sensitivity matching run.

## Deliverables
- symlink resolution to real targets where possible
- traversal detection for `..` escapes
- handling for `/dev/fd/` and `/proc/self/fd/` style indirection
- rejection or safe handling for null bytes and similarly malformed path input

## Out of Scope
- parsing tool input to discover candidate paths
- evaluator policy selection after classification
- CLI rendering

## Acceptance Criteria
- tests cover a symlink into a protected file, traversal attempts, file-descriptor indirection, null-byte input, and paths with spaces or Unicode
- non-existent targets fail gracefully without panic
- hardened output is suitable for downstream allowlist and pack matching without further canonicalization steps in hook mode

title: Add CI workflow for fmt, clippy, and test
description:
## Scope
Add the baseline GitHub Actions workflow for the Rust crate once the scaffold exists.

## Deliverables
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml`
- steps for `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, and `cargo test`
- triggers on push to `main` and on pull requests

## Out of Scope
- release packaging
- Homebrew publishing
- installer validation beyond normal test execution

## Acceptance Criteria
- the workflow YAML is valid and committed in the repo
- CI runs the three required quality gates
- the workflow is wired to `main` pushes and pull requests
- the workflow passes on the scaffold crate without requiring unreleased project files

title: Path extraction from Read, Grep, and Bash tool inputs
description: Implement path extraction for each tool type: Read tool has file_path field, Grep tool has path field, Bash tool requires parsing cat/head/tail/less/python -c/node -e commands to extract target file paths. Normalize relative paths to absolute. Handle quoted paths, glob arguments, piped commands. Model after dcg's context.rs span classification.
close_reason: Split into Read/Grep extraction and Bash extraction for parallelization

title: Installer script: install binary and delegate hook configuration
description:
## Scope
Create the shell installer that downloads the correct `veil` binary and performs the first-run install flow for Claude users.

## Deliverables
- `install.sh` with platform detection for macOS and Linux on arm64 and x86_64
- download and extraction of the correct release artifact
- install to `~/.local/bin/veil`
- delegation to `veil install` for `settings.json` hook mutation rather than duplicating that logic in shell
- support for `--uninstall`, `--status`, `--dry-run`, and `--force`

## Out of Scope
- release workflow creation
- Homebrew formula work
- implementing hook mutation logic inside shell beyond invoking the CLI

## Acceptance Criteria
- the script selects the correct release artifact for each supported platform branch
- dry-run mode reports the actions without mutating disk
- install and uninstall paths are idempotent
- hook mutation flows through the Rust CLI install or uninstall command rather than a second shell-only implementation

title: Extract candidate paths from Read and Grep tool inputs
description:
## Scope
Extract candidate file paths from `Read` and `Grep` tool inputs.

## Deliverables
- `Read` extraction from `file_path`
- `Grep` extraction from `path`
- handling for `output_mode`, where `content` is inspectable and `files_with_matches` does not expose file contents
- relative-to-absolute normalization for extracted paths

## Out of Scope
- Bash command parsing
- symlink and traversal hardening
- policy decisions

## Acceptance Criteria
- unit tests cover `Read` with absolute and relative paths
- `Grep` in `content` mode yields a candidate path for evaluation
- `Grep` in `files_with_matches` mode is treated as non-leaking and does not force a content-read decision
- missing or malformed path fields are handled without panic

comments.author: WhiteHeron
comments.text: Implemented `src/extract.rs` with protocol-neutral Read/Grep candidate-path extraction, relative-to-absolute normalization, and exposure tracking (`ReadsContents` vs `MetadataOnly`). Added unit coverage for Read absolute/relative paths, Grep `content`, Grep `files_with_matches`, malformed JSON, missing path fields, and unsupported tools. Verified with `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo test`, and `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`.,workdir:/Users/zac/Source/cmdrvl/veil,max_output_tokens:4000,yield_time_ms:1000}},{

title: Test suite: unit, integration, golden file, and property tests
description: Comprehensive test coverage: unit tests for each module (path extraction, allowlist, sensitivity, spine detection, config), integration tests for end-to-end hook invocation with JSON stdin, golden file tests for known paths → expected decisions, property tests (allowlisted paths never denied, spine tools never denied, sensitive + no allowlist + no spine → always denied).
close_reason: Tests folded into module beads as acceptance criteria

title: Resolve global policy default and protected-directory semantics
description:
## Scope
Resolve the current mismatch between the README examples and the implementation plan for how `veil` treats unknown files outside protected directories.

## Deliverables
- one canonical rule for global default behavior and protected-directory behavior
- aligned wording in `README.md` and `docs/plan.md`
- code-facing expectations that downstream config and evaluator work can implement against

## Out of Scope
- implementing the evaluator
- changing CLI behavior beyond documenting the contract

## Acceptance Criteria
- `README.md` and `docs/plan.md` describe the same default stance
- the contract covers these three cases: unknown file outside protected dirs, unknown file inside protected dirs, classified-sensitive file
- downstream implementation beads can point to this issue as the source of truth without reopening the design question

comments.author: Zac
comments.text: AmberCompass starting bd-38x. Reserving only README.md and docs/plan.md to resolve the default-policy and protected-directory contract before evaluator/config work lands.

comments.author: AmberCompass
comments.text: Aligned  and  on the canonical default stance. Contract is now explicit: unknown files outside protected directories are allowed, unknown files inside protected directories resolve through , and classified-sensitive files resolve through pack/policy rules.

comments.author: AmberCompass
comments.text: Correction: updated README.md and docs/plan.md so they now share one contract. Unknown files outside protected directories are allowed. Unknown files inside protected directories resolve through [policy] default. Classified-sensitive files resolve through pack-specific rules or [policy] default.

comments.author: Zac
comments.text: AmberCompass reviewed the current doc delta for bd-38x and it satisfies the contract: unknown files outside protected directories are allowed; unknown files inside protected directories resolve through `[policy] default`; classified-sensitive files anywhere resolve through pack-specific rules or `[policy] default`. README and docs/plan now say the same thing.

title: CLI subcommands: test and explain
description:
## Scope
Add the single-path inspection commands that let an operator ask what `veil` would do and why.

## Deliverables
- `veil test <path>` returning the allow or deny decision for one path
- `veil explain <path>` showing the matching allowlist entry, pack, or policy reason
- human-readable and `--json` output modes that use the same evaluator and path-hardening behavior as hook mode

## Out of Scope
- recursive directory scanning
- config and audit introspection
- hook installation commands

## Acceptance Criteria
- both commands operate on a single path without requiring hook stdin input
- `veil test` and `veil explain` have stable human-readable output and `--json` output
- tests cover an allowlisted path, a protected sensitive path, and a path with no sensitivity match
- the commands route paths through the same hardening and evaluator logic used by hook mode

title: Hook output: JSON response, stderr warning, and audit log
description: Implement output formatting: JSON stdout for hook protocol (permissionDecision deny/allow), colorful stderr warning explaining why access was blocked and suggesting spine tool alternatives, JSONL audit log appended to .veil/audit.jsonl with timestamp, tool type, path, decision, reason, sensitivity level, confidence, session ID. Model after dcg's output formatting.
close_reason: Split into output formatting and audit log

title: Implement default hook-mode pipeline
description:
## Scope
Implement the default no-subcommand hook-mode pipeline on top of the shared CLI shell.

## Deliverables
- hook-mode handler invoked by the CLI shell when no subcommand is provided
- stdin read, hook parse, path extraction, path hardening, evaluation, output render, and audit append wiring
- end-to-end integration coverage for the hook-mode path

## Out of Scope
- top-level CLI argument parsing and subcommand dispatch
- utility and operator-facing subcommands
- installer shell script logic
- release workflow automation

## Acceptance Criteria
- running the binary with fixture hook JSON on stdin produces the expected allow and deny responses
- the hook-mode path exercises path hardening before evaluation
- end-to-end tests cover at least one allow and one deny case through the full pipeline
- hook mode plugs into the shared CLI shell without redefining the command surface

title: Evaluate access decisions from normalized inputs
description:
## Scope
Build the evaluator that combines allowlist, sensitivity classification, protected-directory context, spine-tool authorization, and policy selection into one decision.

## Deliverables
- `src/evaluator.rs`
- deterministic evaluation order
- confidence propagation onto the final decision
- fail-open behavior for internal error or timeout paths, with audit-friendly metadata

## Out of Scope
- hook stdin parsing
- stdout or stderr rendering
- audit log persistence itself

## Acceptance Criteria
- integration tests cover allowlisted path, non-sensitive path, sensitive direct read, sensitive spine invocation, and directory-sensitive file outside a protected directory
- internal error or timeout paths fail open and preserve a reason suitable for audit logging
- evaluator output is a single `Decision` value that downstream hook mode can render directly

title: GitHub Actions CI/CD: test, clippy, release workflow
description: Set up .github/workflows/ci.yml (cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy, cargo test) and release.yml (cross-compile for macOS arm/intel + Linux arm/intel, create GitHub Release with tarballs, trigger homebrew formula update). Follow spine tool CI patterns.
close_reason: Splitting into CI workflow (immediate) and release workflow (late)

title: Match allowlisted safe paths as a fast path
description:
## Scope
Implement the safe-path matcher that allows obviously safe files before sensitivity classification runs.

## Deliverables
- compiled default safe patterns
- exact-match, glob, and directory-match support
- a reusable matcher API that downstream evaluation code can call cheaply

## Out of Scope
- config file loading
- sensitivity pack matching
- policy resolution

## Acceptance Criteria
- unit tests cover exact, glob, and directory matches
- the default matcher allows known-safe docs and config files such as `README.md` and `Cargo.toml`
- files such as `credentials.json` and `secrets.yaml` are not allowlisted by default
- the matcher can be called repeatedly without rebuilding pattern state each time

comments.author: AmberCompass
comments.text: AmberCompass starting bd-b0v. Reserving only src/allowlist.rs and tests/allowlist.rs to implement the default safe-path matcher and unit coverage without touching the active src/lib.rs lane.

comments.author: AmberCompass
comments.text: Implemented a reusable safe-path matcher in src/allowlist.rs with compiled default patterns plus exact-name, basename-glob, and directory-prefix matching. Exported the module from src/lib.rs and added tests/allowlist.rs covering exact, glob, directory, default-safe, default-deny, and repeated-call behavior. Validation passed with cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, and cargo test.

title: CLI subcommands: config, audit, and doctor
description:
## Scope
Add the operator introspection commands for resolved config, recent audit history, and installation health.

## Deliverables
- `veil config` showing the merged effective configuration
- `veil audit` showing recent audit log entries
- `veil doctor` validating expected paths, config load, and hook readiness
- human-readable and `--json` output modes

## Out of Scope
- hook installation mutation
- single-path explain logic
- recursive directory classification

## Acceptance Criteria
- each command works without hook stdin input
- `veil config` reflects merged config values from the config subsystem
- `veil audit` reads recent JSONL entries without exposing raw file contents beyond logged metadata
- `veil doctor` reports at least one healthy path and one actionable failure mode in tests or fixtures

title: CLI subcommands: install and uninstall
description:
## Scope
Add the Rust CLI commands that manage Claude hook installation inside `settings.json` so hook mutation logic lives in one place.

## Deliverables
- `veil install` to add `Read`, `Grep`, and `Bash` PreToolUse hook entries
- `veil uninstall` to remove the `veil` hook entries cleanly
- idempotent behavior when hooks are already present or already absent

## Out of Scope
- downloading or placing the binary on disk
- Homebrew formula work
- release workflow packaging

## Acceptance Criteria
- `veil install` adds the expected hook entries without duplicating them on a second run
- `veil uninstall` removes only `veil` owned hook entries
- tests cover install, uninstall, and idempotent re-run behavior against fixture `settings.json` files

comments.author: WhiteHeron
comments.text: Starting bd-nnb. Reserving only src/main.rs, src/cli.rs, and a new install/uninstall helper module to implement Claude settings.json hook mutation with idempotent install/uninstall behavior and focused tests.

title: Add release workflow for multi-platform artifacts
description:
## Scope
Add the release workflow that builds and publishes the multi-platform `veil` binaries used by distribution channels.

## Deliverables
- `.github/workflows/release.yml`
- builds for macOS arm64, macOS x86_64, Linux arm64, and Linux x86_64
- release artifacts whose names are stable enough for the installer script and Homebrew formula to consume

## Out of Scope
- Homebrew formula changes
- shell installer behavior
- general CI on every push

## Acceptance Criteria
- a tag-triggered workflow produces the four expected release artifacts
- artifact names and archive structure are documented or encoded consistently enough for downstream automation
- the workflow depends on the tested hook-mode binary, not an unverified build path
